classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon IV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If any bishop, presbyter, deacon, sub-deacon, lector, cantor, or door-keeper has had intercourse with a woman dedicated to God, let him be deposed, as one who has corrupted a spouse of Christ, but if a layman let him be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A cleric coupled to a spouse of God shall be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: In the case of a layman he shall be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars II., Causa XXVII., Q.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A layman ravishing a nun, by secular law was punished by death.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Balsamon gives the reference thus: V Cap. primi tit. iiij. lib.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
